title: Botox Injection points for smile lines.
filename: Botox Injection points for smile lines..en.vtt
excerpt: So what's the difference between injecting here and here each one of these injection points are commonly injected around the world while attempting to reduce lateral canth lines or smile lines and each one of them will actually produce different results so I've chosen these point…

segments.text: so what's the difference between injecting here and here each one of these injection points are commonly injected around the world while attempting to reduce lateral canth lines or smile lines and each one of them will actually produce different results so I've chosen these points specifically to illustrate the anatomy that lies underneath and how the millimeters really matter when injecting botel toxins so let's have a look at the first injection Point quite close to the eyebrow if we translate this injection Point here over to the anatomical model you'll see that it's actually affecting the frontalis muscle right where the lateral eyebrow is lifted this is a critical area not to treat in most people because the lateral eyebrow is so sensitive to drops if you think about the shape of your face a small drop or a small change in eyebrow movement laterally is very obvious because you've got the other side to compare to you'll either get asymmetry if one side is dropped or it makes both sides look tired it's quite rare that we want to lower someone's eyebrows and

that injection point is perfectly designed to lower an eyebrow and not to affect the lateral canol lines because it's completely mist or bicular oculi if we go down to the next point this is now at the tail of the eyebrow this injection is quite different this one actually is in the bulk of a bicular oculi muscle and this one is correctly placed so long as you inject superficially to release some of the downward pull from the orbicular oculi right where it's pulling on the eyebrow and this is why it is a subtle way of lifting the eyebrow with a small dose of oim toxin usually four units so this injection should actually cause a desired effect so long as we inject at the right depth and go don't go too deep if you were to go deep Too Deep the next closest structure you'd want to avoid might be the lacal L but it is behind the bone so it's not super likely that would be affected the next injection Point down we're still in the same line of the other injections and you might think therefore that it is safer but actually because of the anatomy and how the muscle actually arcs away from the eye at this point we are

now actually missing the bulk of the bicular oculi so if you have a look at this muscle the bulk of the muscle is actually lateral to that injection point we're now to medial to the eye and this is now placing us very close to another potential muscle that we don't want to treat which is the lateral rectus muscle if you get balim toxin into the muscle that causes the eye to look away from the midline to look laterally then you get double vision when the patient looks in that direction and that's because that muscle is weakened by botul toxin so this injection point is too close to the orbital rim and what we need to do is inject more laterally to keep it safe so I hope that makes it clear there are three similar injection points one causes a brow drop one causes an eyebrow lift and the other one causes a lateral rectus py if you inject enough at the right depth so if you'd like to see more of these style of videos let me know in the comments and I will go over another area of the face also don't forget to sign up to the waiting list for my New Movement if you're interested in learning anatomy and Bim toxin injections in extraordinary 3D detail
then that's the place to do it it's not open yet but you can join the waiting list
